i ran the audit on the project i inspected it and i found these issues that deviate from the origional goal of the project act as a senior and fix them all here are the issues Conclusion: **Fail**
- Rationale:
  - Reconciliation core requirement is only partially implemented: records are read/updated but never generated from orders/issues/events in backend routes.

 Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
- Rationale:
  - Covered: local auth/session/reset; role-aware UI; profile/address/default; reviews/followup/image limits; after-sales statuses with SLA fields; content metadata + sensitive words; file type/signature/size checks; offline payment modes + idempotency.

- Conclusion: **Fail**
- Rationale:
  - High alignment on most offline-first and domain features.
  - But core fulfillment reconciliation requirement is not fully implemented and cannot be proven complete statically.
  - RBAC scope includes academic staff user deactivation capability, which conflicts with prompt’s role semantics centered on admins for governance controls.
- Evidence: reconciliation endpoints only read/update (`repo/backend/src/routes/orders.rs:429-472`); academic staff granted `users.manage` in seed + enforced by route permission (`repo/backend/src/migrations/002_seed.sql:55-57`, `repo/backend/src/routes/users.rs:122-127`).

**Severity: High**
- Title: Frontend sign-out does not invalidate server-side session
- Conclusion: Fail
- Evidence: `repo/frontend/src/components/nav.rs:31`, `repo/frontend/src/services/auth.rs:76-79`, backend logout endpoint exists at `repo/backend/src/routes/auth.rs:461-469`

Title: Reconciliation workflow is incomplete (records never generated)
- Conclusion: Fail
- Evidence: only select/update reconciliation records (`repo/backend/src/routes/orders.rs:443-467`), table exists (`repo/backend/src/migrations/001_initial.sql:181-194`), no insert path in backend (`repo/backend/src/routes/orders.rs`, search results)

- Title: RBAC scope drift allows academic staff to deactivate users
- Conclusion: Fail
- Evidence: staff granted `users.manage` (`repo/backend/src/migrations/002_seed.sql:55-57`), route enforces `users.manage` for deactivation (`repo/backend/src/routes/users.rs:122-127`)

Title: Delivery docs are internally inconsistent with implemented API surface
- Conclusion: Fail
- Evidence: top-level API spec includes non-existent contracts (`docs/api-spec.md:23-55`, `162-237`) while implemented routes differ (`repo/backend/src/routes/mod.rs:16-137`)

 **Severity: Medium**
- Title: Login/provision may return success even if session insert fails
- Conclusion: Partial Fail
- Evidence: ignored DB execution result on session insertion in login/provision (`repo/backend/src/routes/auth.rs:103-113`, `176-185`)

**Severity: Medium**
- Title: Guided submission templates are not implemented
- Conclusion: Partial Fail
- Evidence: submission creation only accepts generic fields (`repo/backend/src/models/submission.rs:39-47`), UI form has no template selection/workflow (`repo/frontend/src/pages/submissions.rs:227-313`)

 **Severity: Medium**
- Title: Frontend admin/protected routes are not client-guarded
- Conclusion: Partial Pass
- Evidence: routes render pages unconditionally (`repo/frontend/src/main.rs:376-401`)

**Severity: Medium**
- Title: Reconciliation SLA/scheduler timing semantics not aligned to documented midnight schedule
- Conclusion: Partial Fail
- Evidence: scheduler runs every 24h from process start (`repo/backend/src/main.rs:35-37`), docs claim nightly schedule semantics (`docs/questions.md:78-79`)

 **Severity: Low**
- Title: Conflicting session timeout defaults in compose/env vs runtime constant usage
- Conclusion: Partial Pass
- Evidence: compose sets `SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=60` (`repo/docker-compose.yml:35`), `.env.example` says 30 (`repo/.env.example:5`), runtime uses compile-time constant 30 in auth flow (`repo/backend/src/models/mod.
I'll start by reading all the relevant files to understand the codebase before making fixes.

Now I have all the information needed. Let me provide a comprehensive analysis of the Meridian Academy project:

## Meridian Academy - Complete Project Analysis

### 1. Project Structure Overview

The Meridian Academy project is a full-stack academic publishing and fulfillment platform built with:
- **Backend**: Rust + Rocket web framework + MySQL database
- **Frontend**: Rust + Dioxus (WASM framework) 
- **Containerization**: Docker + Docker Compose

Directory structure:
```
/repo/
├── backend/          # Rocket REST API server
│   └── src/
│       ├── main.rs           # Server initialization & routing
│       ├── lib.rs
│       ├── routes/           # API endpoint handlers
│       ├── models/           # Data structures & business logic
│       ├── middleware/       # Auth & request processing
│       └── migrations/       # SQL schema & seed data
├── frontend/         # Dioxus WASM UI
│   └── src/
│       ├── main.rs          # Router & page components
│       ├── services/        # API client & auth
│       ├── components/      # Reusable UI components
│       └── pages/           # Feature pages
├── docker-compose.yml       # Local dev environment
├── .env.example            # Configuration template
└── docs/
    ├── api-spec.md         # REST endpoint documentation
    └── questions.md        # Q&A clarifications
```

---

### 2. Backend Routes (/repo/backend/src/routes/)

**File: mod.rs** - Route registration hub that mounts all route modules:
- Health check routes
- Auth routes (login, registration, password reset, logout, data export)
- User routes (CRUD, addresses, notifications)
- Submission routes (create, list, version control, download)
- Order routes (create, split/merge, fulfillment tracking, reconciliation)
- Review routes (create, follow-up, image uploads)
- Case routes (create, status updates, comments, SLA tracking)
- Payment routes (create, refund, reconciliation reporting)
- Admin routes (dashboard, audit logs, system settings)
- Content routes (sensitive word management, content governance)

**Key Routes Summary:**
- **auth.rs** (471 lines): Login, password reset, account deletion, data export
- **users.rs** (228 lines): User management, addresses, notifications
- **submissions.rs**: Version control for academic submissions with deadline enforcement
- **orders.rs**: Subscription orders with line items, fulfillment events, reconciliation
- **reviews.rs**: Post-purchase reviews with 5-star ratings, 14-day follow-up window
- **cases.rs**: After-sales case management with SLA timers (2-day first response, 7-day resolution)
- **payments.rs**: Idempotent payment processing (charge, hold, release, refund)
- **admin.rs**: Dashboard stats, audit logs, system settings
- **content.rs**: Sensitive word dictionary (replace/block policies), content governance
- **health.rs**: Health check endpoint for Docker healthchecks

---

### 3. Backend Models (/repo/backend/src/models/)

**File: mod.rs** - Core business logic & constants:
- `AppConfig`: JWT secret, token expiry, session timeout configuration
- `generate_seo()`: Deterministic SEO metadata generation (title, description, slug)
- `validate_metadata()`: Content metadata length validation
- `validate_file_type()`: Magic byte verification for PDF, DOCX, PNG, JPG
- **Constants**:
  - `MAX_FILE_SIZE`: 25 MB
  - `MAX_REVIEW_IMAGE_SIZE`: 5 MB per image
  - `MAX_REVIEW_IMAGES`: 6 images per review
  - `FOLLOWUP_WINDOW_DAYS`: 14 days
  - `SLA_FIRST_RESPONSE_HOURS`: 48 hours
  - `SLA_RESOLUTION_HOURS`: 168 hours (7 days)
  - `SOFT_DELETE_HOLD_DAYS`: 30 days
  - `PASSWORD_RESET_EXPIRY_MINUTES`: 60 minutes
  - `SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES`: 30 minutes
  - `MAX_SUBMISSION_VERSIONS`: 10
  - `HIGH_QUANTITY_THRESHOLD`: 50 units
  - `REFUND_COUNT_THRESHOLD`: 3 refunds in 30 days
- `add_business_days()`: Adds N business days (Mon-Fri only)
- `valid_case_transition()`: Validates after-sales case status transitions

**File: user.rs**:
```
User: id, username, email, password_hash, first_name, last_name, contact_info, 
       role, is_active, soft_deleted_at, deletion_scheduled_at, invoice_title, 
       notification_flags, timestamps

UserAddress: id, user_id, label, street_line1, street_line2, city, state, zip_code, is_default

NotificationItem: id, title, message, is_read, created_at
```

**File: auth.rs**:
```
LoginRequest: username, password
LoginResponse: token, user
Claims (JWT): sub, username, role, exp, iat, session_id
ChangePasswordRequest: current_password, new_password
GenerateResetTokenRequest: user_id
ResetTokenResponse: token, expires_at
UseResetTokenRequest: token, new_password
ExportDataResponse: user_profile, addresses, submissions, orders, reviews, cases, exported_at
```

**File: order.rs**:
```
Order: id, user_id, order_number, subscription_period (monthly|quarterly|annual),
       shipping_address_id, status, payment_status, total_amount, parent_order_id,
       is_flagged, flag_reason, timestamps

OrderLineItem: id, order_id, publication_title, series_name, quantity, unit_price, line_total

FulfillmentEvent: id, order_id, line_item_id, event_type (missing_issue|reshipment|delay|
                  discontinuation|edition_change|delivered), issue_identifier, reason, 
                  expected_date, actual_date, logged_by, created_at

ReconciliationRecord: id, order_id, line_item_id, issue_identifier, expected_qty, received_qty,
                      status (pending|matched|discrepancy), notes
```

**File: submission.rs**:
```
Submission: id, author_id, title, summary, submission_type, status (draft|submitted|in_review|
            revision_requested|accepted|rejected|published|blocked), deadline, current_version,
            max_versions, meta_title, meta_description, slug, tags, keywords, timestamps

SubmissionVersion: id, submission_id, version_number, file_name, file_path, file_size, file_type,
                   file_hash, magic_bytes, form_data, submitted_at

SubmitVersionRequest: file_name, file_data (base64), form_data
```

**File: review.rs**:
```
Review: id, order_id, line_item_id, user_id, rating (1-5), title (max 120 chars), body,
        is_followup, parent_review_id, timestamps

ReviewImage: id, review_id, file_name, file_path, file_size
```

**File: case.rs**:
```
AfterSalesCase: id, order_id, reporter_id, assigned_to, case_type (return|refund|exchange),
                subject, description, status (submitted|in_review|awaiting_evidence|arbitrated|
                approved|denied|closed), priority (low|medium|high|urgent),
                submitted_at, first_response_at, first_response_due, resolution_target,
                resolved_at, closed_at, timestamps

CaseWithSla: case + first_response_overdue, resolution_overdue, hours_until_first_response,
             hours_until_resolution (computed fields)

CaseComment: id, case_id, author_id, content, created_at
```

**File: payment.rs**:
```
Payment: id, order_id, idempotency_key (UNIQUE), payment_method (cash|check|on_account),
         amount, transaction_type (charge|hold|release|refund), reference_payment_id,
         status (pending|completed|held|released|refunded|failed), check_number, notes,
         processed_by, timestamps

ReconciliationReport: id, report_date, expected_balance, actual_balance, discrepancy, details

AbnormalOrderFlag: id, order_id, user_id, flag_type (high_quantity|repeated_refunds|manual),
                   reason, is_cleared, cleared_by, cleared_at, created_at
```

**File: content.rs**:
```
SensitiveWord: id, word, action (replace|block), replacement, added_by

ContentCheckResult: is_blocked, blocked_words[], processed_text, replacements_made[]

check_sensitive_words(): Runs case-insensitive match against sensitive word dictionary
- "block" action: prevents saving if word found
- "replace" action: replaces with configured string (default: "***")
```

---

### 4. Backend main.rs

**File: /repo/backend/src/main.rs** (130 lines)

Complete Rocket server initialization:

1. **Macro setup**: `#[macro_use] extern crate rocket`

2. **Database initialization**:
   - Reads `DATABASE_URL` from environment
   - Creates MySQL connection pool with max 10 connections
   - Runs migrations (001_initial.sql, 002_seed.sql) with idempotent skip logic

3. **Background jobs**:
   - Spawns async task that runs `generate_reconciliation_report()` every 24 hours at midnight

4. **CORS configuration**:
   - Whitelists specific local origins: FRONTEND_URL env var, localhost:8080, 127.0.0.1:8080
   - Enables credentials
   - Prevents unauthorized cross-origin requests

5. **Admin password seeding**:
   - If `ADMIN_PASSWORD` env var set, bcrypt hashes it and updates all seeded accounts:
     - admin, moderator, host, clerk, member

6. **Route mounting**:
   - `/health` - health check
   - `/api/auth` - auth endpoints
   - `/api/users` - user management
   - `/api/submissions` - submission management
   - `/api/orders` - order management
   - `/api/reviews` - reviews
   - `/api/cases` - after-sales cases
   - `/api/payments` - payments
   - `/api/admin` - admin endpoints
   - `/api/content` - content governance

---

### 5. Frontend main.rs

**File: /repo/frontend/src/main.rs** (402 lines)

Dioxus WASM single-page application:

**Routes**:
```
Home (/) - Landing page with feature cards
Login (/login) - Sign-in form
AdminProvision (/admin/provision) - Create accounts (admin-only)
Dashboard (/dashboard) - Role-specific dashboard
Submissions (/submissions, /submissions/new) - View/create submissions
Orders (/orders, /orders/new) - View/create orders
Reviews (/reviews) - Post-purchase reviews
Cases (/cases, /cases/new) - After-sales case management
Admin (/admin, /admin/users) - Administration panel
Profile (/profile) - User profile management
```

**Key Components**:
- **Home**: Displays features, login/signup CTAs
- **Login**: Username/password form, error display
- **AdminProvision**: Create new user accounts with role selection
- **Dashboard**: Role-based widget display:
  - Students/Instructors: Submissions card
  - All: Orders, Cases cards
  - Instructors/Staff: Reviews card
  - Admins: Admin panel card
- **Page wrapper**: Each route wraps content in `Layout` component

---

### 6. Frontend services/auth.rs

**File: /repo/frontend/src/services/auth.rs** (84 lines)

Client-side authentication service:

```rust
UserInfo {
    id, username, email, first_name, last_name, role, is_active,
    invoice_title, notify_submissions, notify_orders, notify_reviews, notify_cases
}

RegisterData {
    username, email, password, first_name, last_name, role
}
```

**Functions**:
- `login(username, password)`: Calls `/api/auth/login`, stores token & user in localStorage
- `register(data)`: Calls `/api/auth/provision`, stores token & user
- `get_current_user()`: Retrieves user from localStorage
- `get_token()`: Retrieves auth token
- `logout()`: Clears localStorage
- `is_authenticated()`: Checks if token exists

---

### 7. Frontend components/nav.rs

**File: /repo/frontend/src/components/nav.rs** (44 lines)

Navigation bar component:

```rust
#[component]
pub fn NavBar() -> Element {
    // Checks auth state with get_current_user()
    // Renders conditional nav links based on user role:
    
    // Authenticated users see:
    - Dashboard
    - Submissions (student/instructor only)
    - Orders
    - Reviews
    - Cases
    - Admin (administrators only)
    - Profile (with user's first_name as label)
    - Sign Out button with logout() call
    
    // Unauthenticated users see:
    - Sign In link
    // (No public registration — admin-provisioned only)
}
```

---

### 8. Migration Files

**File: 001_initial.sql** (379 lines)

Complete database schema:

**Core Tables**:
- `users` (18 fields): Roles, soft delete, notification prefs
- `sessions` (6 fields): Token-based sessions with expiry
- `password_reset_tokens` (7 fields): One-time reset tokens
- `audit_log` (7 fields): Immutable action log
- `notifications` (5 fields): In-app notifications

**Content/Submissions**:
- `submissions` (14 fields): Title, status, deadline, version control, SEO metadata
- `submission_versions` (10 fields): File storage with magic bytes

**Orders & Fulfillment**:
- `orders` (13 fields): Order number, subscription period, status, flags
- `order_line_items` (7 fields): Per-publication line items
- `fulfillment_events` (9 fields): Shipment tracking
- `reconciliation_records` (8 fields): Expected vs received qty

**Reviews & Cases**:
- `reviews` (10 fields): Rating, followup tracking, parent reference
- `review_images` (6 fields): Image attachments (up to 5 MB each)
- `after_sales_cases` (16 fields): SLA timers, priority levels
- `case_comments` (4 fields): Case discussion thread
- `case_evidence` (6 fields): File attachments

**Payments & Admin**:
- `payments` (13 fields): Idempotency keys, transaction types, status
- `reconciliation_reports` (6 fields): Nightly report snapshots
- `abnormal_order_flags` (8 fields): Manual/auto-flagged orders
- `sensitive_words` (5 fields): Content governance dictionary
- `roles` (4 fields): Role definitions
- `permissions` (4 fields): Permission catalog
- `role_permissions` (5 fields): Role→Permission mapping

**Alterations**:
- Adds `file_data MEDIUMBLOB` to submission_versions
- Adds `file_type`, `file_hash`, `image_data MEDIUMBLOB` to review_images

**File: 002_seed.sql** (92 lines)

Seed data:
- **Admin user**: ID `admin-0001-0001-0001-000000000001`, username `admin`, role `administrator`
- **Roles**: student, instructor, academic_staff, administrator
- **Permissions**: 20 permissions covering users, submissions, orders, reviews, cases, payments, content, admin
- **Role mappings**: 
  - Student: create submissions, orders, reviews, cases
  - Instructor: create submissions, review submissions, manage orders, cases, reviews
  - Academic Staff: list/manage users, submissions, orders, cases, payments, fulfillment
  - Administrator: full access to all operations
- **Sensitive words**: 3 examples (badword→replace, forbidden→block, offensive→replace)

---

### 9. docker-compose.yml

**File: /repo/docker-compose.yml** (74 lines)

Local development environment with 3 services:

**MySQL Service**:
- Image: mysql:8
- Container: meridian_mysql
- Port: 3306
- Database: meridian_academy (configurable)
- User: meridian_user (configurable)
- Password: meridian_pass (configurable)
- Volume: mysql_data (persistent)
- Health check: mysqladmin ping

**Backend Service**:
- Build: ./backend/Dockerfile
- Container: meridian_backend
- Port: 8000
- Environment:
  - DATABASE_URL: mysql://meridian_user:meridian_pass@mysql:3306/meridian_academy
  - ROCKET_PORT: 8000
  - ROCKET_ADDRESS: 0.0.0.0
  - ROCKET_SECRET_KEY: Random 32+ char key (change for production)
  - JWT_SECRET: Long secret key
  - TOKEN_EXPIRY_HOURS: 24
  - SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES: 60
  - ADMIN_PASSWORD: admin123
  - RUST_LOG: info
- Depends on: mysql (healthy)
- Health check: curl http://localhost:8000/health

**Frontend Service**:
- Build: ./frontend/Dockerfile
- Container: meridian_frontend
- Port: 8080
- Environment:
  - BACKEND_URL: http://backend:8000
- Depends on: backend (healthy)

**Networks**: Single bridge network (meridian_net) for inter-container communication

---

### 10. .env.example

**File: /repo/.env.example** (18 lines)

Environment variables template:

```
DATABASE_URL=mysql://meridian_user:meridian_pass@localhost:3306/meridian_academy
ROCKET_PORT=8000
ROCKET_ADDRESS=0.0.0.0
ROCKET_SECRET_KEY=random_secret_key_change_in_production_at_least_32_bytes_long!!
SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=30
TOKEN_EXPIRY_HOURS=24
JWT_SECRET=change_this_jwt_secret_in_production_must_be_long
PASSWORD_RESET_EXPIRY_MINUTES=60
SOFT_DELETE_HOLD_DAYS=30
MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=rootpassword
MYSQL_DATABASE=meridian_academy
MYSQL_USER=meridian_user
MYSQL_PASSWORD=meridian_pass
FRONTEND_URL=http://localhost:8080
BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:8000
RUST_LOG=info
ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin123
```

---

### 11. backend/src/routes/mod.rs

**File: /repo/backend/src/routes/mod.rs** (139 lines)

Route module coordinator:

**Submodules**: admin, auth, cases, content, health, orders, payments, reviews, submissions, users

**Route functions**:

1. `health_routes()`: 
   - GET /health → health_check

2. `auth_routes()`: (9 endpoints)
   - POST /login, /provision, /change-password, /generate-reset-token, /use-reset-token
   - POST /request-deletion, /cancel-deletion, /logout
   - GET /me, /export-my-data

3. `user_routes()`: (12 endpoints)
   - GET /list_users, /:id, /notifications, /addresses
   - PUT /profile, /notification-prefs, /:id/role, /addresses/default, /addresses/:addr_id
   - POST /addresses, /notifications/:notif_id/read
   - DELETE /:id, /addresses/:addr_id

4. `submission_routes()`: (9 endpoints)
   - POST / (create), /:id/versions (submit version)
   - GET / (list), /:id, /my, /:id/versions
   - PUT /:id
   - GET /:id/versions/:version/download
   - POST /:id/approve-blocked

5. `order_routes()`: (12 endpoints)
   - POST / (create), /:id/split, /merge, /:id/fulfillment-events
   - GET / (list), /my, /:id, /:id/reconciliation
   - PUT /:id/status, /:id/reconciliation
   - POST /:id/clear-flag
   - GET /flagged

6. `review_routes()`: (6 endpoints)
   - POST / (create), /:id/followup, /:id/images
   - GET / (list), /my, /:id

7. `case_routes()`: (8 endpoints)
   - POST / (create), /:id/assign, /:id/comments
   - GET / (list), /my, /:id, /:id/comments
   - PUT /:id/status

8. `payment_routes()`: (6 endpoints)
   - POST / (create), /refund
   - GET / (list), /:id, /reconciliation
   - POST /:id/clear-flag

9. `admin_routes()`: (5 endpoints)
   - GET /dashboard, /audit-log, /audit-logs, /settings
   - POST /cleanup-soft-deleted

10. `content_routes()`: (10 endpoints)
    - GET /sensitive-words
    - POST /sensitive-words, /check, /items/:id/submit, /items/:id/approve, /items/:id/reject, /items/:id/request-revision, /items/:id/publish
    - DELETE /sensitive-words/:id, /items/:id/rollback/:version

---

## Summary of Key Features

### Authentication & Authorization
- JWT-based token authentication with 24-hour expiry (configurable)
- Session management with 30-minute idle timeout
- Role-based access control (4 roles: student, instructor, academic_staff, administrator)
- Permission-based authorization with 20 granular permissions
- Admin account provisioning only (no public self-registration)
- Password reset tokens with 60-minute expiry
- Soft-delete with 30-day hold before permanent deletion

### Content Management
- Submission versioning (max 10 versions)
- Deadline enforcement server-side
- SEO metadata generation (title, description, slug)
- Sensitive word dictionary with replace/block policies
- Content governance workflow (draft → submitted → in_review → revision_requested → accepted → published)
- File magic byte validation (PDF, DOCX, PNG, JPG)

### Orders & Fulfillment
- Subscription orders (monthly, quarterly, annual)
- Line item management
- Fulfillment event tracking (missing, reshipment, delay, discontinuation, edition_change)
- Reconciliation (expected vs. received quantities)
- Order split/merge operations
- Abnormal order flagging (high quantity >50 units, 3+ refunds in 30 days)

### Reviews & After-Sales
- 5-star post-purchase reviews (max 120 char title)
- 14-day follow-up window (one follow-up per review)
- Review image attachments (6 images max, 5 MB each)
- After-sales case management (return, refund, exchange)
- Case status workflow with SLA timers:
  - 2-day first response target
  - 7-day resolution target
  - SLA pauses when awaiting evidence
- Case assignment and commenting

### Payments
- Multiple payment methods (cash, check, on_account)
- Idempotent payment processing (prevents duplicate charges)
- Transaction types: charge, hold, release, refund
- Nightly reconciliation reports (auto at midnight)
- Abnormal order flag tracking and clearing

### Administration
- Dashboard with stats (users, submissions, orders, cases, revenue, etc.)
- Immutable audit logs
- System settings endpoint
- Soft-delete cleanup scheduling
- User role management with audit trail
- Sensitive word dictionary management

### Data Export
- GDPR-compliant user data export
- Includes: profile, addresses, submissions, orders, reviews, cases
- JSON archive format

---

## Technology Stack Summary

- **Backend**: Rocket (Rust web framework), SQLx (async SQL), Bcrypt, JWT, MySQL 8
- **Frontend**: Dioxus (WASM), Router, Local Storage, gloo-storage
- **Database**: MySQL 8 with 20+ tables
- **Deployment**: Docker & Docker Compose

1	# Meridian Academy — REST API Specification
2	
3	All endpoints are served by the Rocket backend at `http://localhost:8000/api`.
4	
5	Authentication uses a session token passed as a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Auth
10	
11	### POST /api/auth/login
12	Login with username and password.
13	
14	Request: `{ "username": string, "password": string }`
15	Response: `{ "token": string, "user": { "id", "username", "role" } }`
16	Errors: 401 (invalid credentials), 423 (account locked)
17	
18	### POST /api/auth/logout
19	Invalidate the current session token.
20	
21	Response: `{ "success": true }`
22	
23	### POST /api/auth/reset-password
24	Use a one-time reset token to set a new password.
25	
26	Request: `{ "token": string, "new_password": string }`
27	Response: `{ "success": true }`
28	Errors: 400 (token expired or invalid)
29	
30	### GET /api/auth/me
31	Get the current authenticated user.
32	
33	Response: `{ "id", "username", "role", "email" }`
34	
35	---
36	
37	## Users
38	
39	### GET /api/users
40	Admin only. List all users with pagination.
41	
42	Query: `?page=1&per_page=20&role=student`
43	
44	### GET /api/users/:id
45	Get user profile. Users can only access their own; admins can access any.
46	
47	### PUT /api/users/:id
48	Update profile fields (name, email).
49	
50	### DELETE /api/users/:id
51	Soft-delete user account (30-day hold).
52	
53	### GET /api/users/:id/export
54	Export all of the user's own data as a downloadable JSON archive.
55	
56	### GET /api/users/:id/addresses
57	List shipping addresses for a user.
58	
59	### POST /api/users/:id/addresses
60	Add a new shipping address.
61	
62	### PUT /api/users/:id/addresses/:addr_id
63	Update an address. Set `"is_default": true` to make it the default.
64	
65	### DELETE /api/users/:id/addresses/:addr_id
66	Remove an address.
67	
68	---
69	
70	## Submissions
71	
72	### GET /api/submissions
73	List submissions. Students see own; instructors see their courses'; admins see all.
74	
75	### POST /api/submissions
76	Create a new submission draft.
77	
78	Request: `{ "title": string, "template_id": string, "deadline": string }`
79	
80	### GET /api/submissions/:id
81	Get submission with full version history.
82	
83	### POST /api/submissions/:id/versions
84	Submit a new version (resubmit). Max 10 versions before deadline.
85	
86	Request: multipart form with fields + file attachments.
87	
88	### GET /api/submissions/:id/versions/:version/download
89	Download a specific version. Response includes watermark metadata in headers.
90	
91	---
92	
93	## Orders
94	
95	### GET /api/orders
96	List orders for the current user (or all for admin).
97	
98	### POST /api/orders
99	Create a new subscription order.
100	
101	Request: `{ "period": "monthly|quarterly|annual", "lines": [{ "series_id", "quantity" }] }`
102	
103	### GET /api/orders/:id
104	Get order details with line items and fulfillment events.
105	
106	### POST /api/orders/:id/split
107	Split an order containing multiple series into separate orders.
108	
109	### POST /api/orders/merge
110	Merge multiple orders from the same subscriber.
111	
112	Request: `{ "order_ids": [string] }`
113	
114	### GET /api/orders/:id/reconciliation
115	Get reconciliation view: expected vs. actual receipt per issue.
116	
117	### POST /api/orders/:id/fulfillment-events
118	Log a fulfillment event.
119	
120	Request: `{ "event_type": "missing|reshipment|delay|discontinuation|edition_change", "issue_id": string, "reason": string }`
121	
122	---
123	
124	## Reviews
125	
126	### GET /api/reviews
127	List reviews. Users see own; admins see all.
128	
129	### POST /api/reviews
130	Post an initial review.
131	
132	Request: multipart with `{ "order_id", "rating", "body" }` + up to 6 images (5 MB each).
133	
134	### POST /api/reviews/:id/followup
135	Post a follow-up (within 14 days of original review, one per review).
136	
137	### GET /api/reviews/:id
138	Get review with follow-up and images.
139	
140	---
141	
142	## After-Sales Cases
143	
144	### GET /api/cases
145	List after-sales cases for current user (or all for admin/staff).
146	
147	### POST /api/cases
148	Open a new case.
149	
150	Request: `{ "order_id": string, "case_type": "return|refund|exchange", "description": string }`
151	
152	### GET /api/cases/:id
153	Get case details with status history and SLA timers.
154	
155	### PUT /api/cases/:id/status
156	Update case status (staff/admin only).
157	
158	Request: `{ "status": "in_review|awaiting_evidence|arbitrated|approved|denied|closed", "note": string }`
159	
160	---
161	
162	## Content
163	
164	### GET /api/content
165	List content items.
166	
167	### POST /api/content
168	Create a content draft. Triggers metadata governance on save.
169	
170	Request: `{ "title": string, "summary": string, "body": string, "tags": [string] }`
171	
172	### PUT /api/content/:id
173	Update content. Re-runs governance validation.
174	
175	### POST /api/content/:id/submit
176	Submit for review (Academic Staff approval required if flagged).
177	
178	### POST /api/content/:id/approve
179	Academic Staff or Admin approves and publishes content.
180	
181	### POST /api/content/:id/reject
182	Reject content with a reason.
183	
184	### GET /api/content/:id/versions
185	Get version history.
186	
187	### POST /api/content/:id/rollback/:version
188	Roll back to a previous version.
189	
190	---
191	
192	## Payments
193	
194	### GET /api/payments
195	List payment transactions (admin only).
196	
197	### POST /api/payments
198	Record a new transaction.
199	
200	Request: `{ "order_id": string, "method": "cash|check|on_account", "amount_cents": number, "idempotency_key": string }`
201	
202	### POST /api/payments/:id/refund
203	Issue a refund against a prior transaction.
204	
205	### GET /api/payments/reconciliation
206	Get nightly reconciliation report.
207	
208	---
209	
210	## Admin
211	
212	### GET /api/admin/users
213	List all users with roles and status.
214	
215	### POST /api/admin/users/:id/reset-token
216	Generate a one-time password reset token (60-minute expiry).
217	
218	### PUT /api/admin/users/:id/role
219	Change a user's role. Logged to audit trail.
220	
221	### GET /api/admin/audit-logs
222	Get immutable audit log entries with pagination.
223	
224	### GET /api/admin/sensitive-words
225	List sensitive words in the local dictionary.
226	
227	### POST /api/admin/sensitive-words
228	Add a sensitive word with policy (replace or block).
229	
230	### DELETE /api/admin/sensitive-words/:id
231	Remove a sensitive word.
232	
233	### GET /api/admin/flagged-orders
234	List orders flagged for manual review (abnormal patterns).
235	
236	### POST /api/admin/flagged-orders/:id/clear
237	Clear a flag and allow the order to proceed.
238	
239	---
240	
241	## Health
242	
243	### GET /health
244	Returns 200 OK. Used by Docker healthcheck.

1	# Meridian Academy — Clarifying Questions & Answers
2	
3	## Authentication & Accounts
4	
5	**Q: Can users self-register, or does an admin create accounts?**
6	A: Administrators create accounts and assign roles. There is no public self-registration to prevent unauthorized access to the academic system.
7	
8	**Q: What happens after 30 days of soft-delete hold?**
9	A: The account and all associated data are permanently deleted. A background job or admin-triggered cleanup handles this. The user cannot log in during the hold period.
10	
11	**Q: Can a user have multiple roles simultaneously?**
12	A: No. Each user has exactly one role. Administrators can change a user's role, and the change is logged in the audit trail.
13	
14	**Q: What does "Export My Data" include?**
15	A: It includes the user's own profile, submissions, orders, reviews, after-sales cases, and notification history. It does not include other users' data, system configuration, or audit logs of other users' actions.
16	
17	## Submissions
18	
19	**Q: What happens if a user tries to submit an 11th version?**
20	A: The system rejects the submission with a clear error message indicating the maximum of 10 resubmissions has been reached.
21	
22	**Q: What happens if a user tries to submit after the deadline?**
23	A: The submission is rejected. The deadline is enforced server-side, not just in the UI.
24	
25	**Q: Are watermarks applied to the original file or generated on download?**
26	A: Watermarks are applied at download time. The stored file is the original. The watermark (requester name + timestamp) is overlaid when the file is served.
27	
28	**Q: Can instructors see all student submissions or only those in their courses?**
29	A: Instructors can only see submissions associated with courses they are assigned to. Administrators can see all submissions.
30	
31	## Orders & Fulfillment
32	
33	**Q: Can a single order span multiple subscription periods?**
34	A: No. Each order has one subscription period (monthly, quarterly, or annual). Users create separate orders for different periods.
35	
36	**Q: What triggers the abnormal-order flag?**
37	A: The system flags orders with quantities significantly above the account's historical average, or accounts that have submitted more than 3 refund requests in a 30-day window. Thresholds are configurable by administrators.
38	
39	**Q: Can a merged order be split again later?**
40	A: Yes. Split and merge are reversible operations as long as the order has not been fully fulfilled.
41	
42	## Reviews & After-Sales
43	
44	**Q: Can a user post a review before receiving the order?**
45	A: No. Reviews are linked to fulfilled orders. The review option only appears after at least one fulfillment event is logged for the order.
46	
47	**Q: What happens if the 14-day follow-up window expires?**
48	A: The follow-up option is hidden and the endpoint returns 403. The original review remains visible.
49	
50	**Q: Who can move an after-sales case to "Arbitrated" status?**
51	A: Only Academic Staff and Administrators can set the Arbitrated status, indicating the case has been escalated for a formal decision.
52	
53	**Q: Are SLA timers paused when a case is in "Awaiting Evidence" status?**
54	A: Yes. The SLA clock pauses when the case enters Awaiting Evidence and resumes when evidence is submitted or the status changes.
55	
56	## Content Governance
57	
58	**Q: Who manages the sensitive-word dictionary?**
59	A: Only Administrators can add, edit, or remove entries from the sensitive-word dictionary.
60	
61	**Q: What is the "replace" policy for sensitive words?**
62	A: The matched word or phrase is automatically replaced with a configurable placeholder (e.g., "[REDACTED]") and the content can still be saved and submitted for review.
63	
64	**Q: What is the "block" policy?**
65	A: The content cannot be saved at all until the sensitive word is removed. The user sees an error indicating which word triggered the block.
66	
67	**Q: How are SEO fields generated deterministically?**
68	A: Meta title = first 60 characters of the content title. Meta description = first 160 characters of the summary. Slug = lowercase title with spaces replaced by hyphens and special characters removed.
69	
70	## Payments
71	
72	**Q: What does "idempotent posting" mean in practice?**
73	A: Each transaction submission includes an `idempotency_key`. If the same key is submitted twice, the second request returns the result of the first without creating a duplicate transaction.
74	
75	**Q: What is an "escrow hold"?**
76	A: A hold reserves funds against an account balance without finalizing the transaction. The hold can be released (converting to a completed transaction) or cancelled (returning the funds).
77	
78	**Q: When does the nightly reconciliation run?**
79	A: It runs at midnight local server time. Administrators can also trigger it manually from the admin console.
80	
81	## Deployment
82	
83	**Q: Does the system require internet access?**
84	A: No. The entire system runs on a local network. MySQL, the Rocket backend, and the Dioxus frontend all run in Docker containers on the same machine or local network.
85	
86	**Q: Can multiple users use the system simultaneously?**
87	A: Yes. The Rocket backend handles concurrent requests. MySQL manages concurrent database access. The system is designed for multi-user institutional use.

{"file_path": "/home/eren/Documents/task4/Meridian_Academy/docs/api-spec.md", "content": "# Meridian Academy — REST API Specification

All endpoints are served by the Rocket backend at `http://localhost:8000`.

Authentication uses a JWT token passed as a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header.

---

## Health

### GET /health
Returns 200 OK. Used by Docker healthcheck.

---

## Auth

### POST /api/auth/login
Login with username and password.

Request: `{ "username": string, "password": string }`
Response: `{ "token": string, "user": UserResponse }`
Errors: 401 (invalid credentials)

### POST /api/auth/provision
Admin-provisioned account creation. Requires `admin.provision_users` permission.

Request: `{ "username": string, "email": string, "password": string, "first_name": string, "last_name": string, "role": string }`
Response: `{ "token": string, "user": UserResponse }`
Errors: 403 (not admin), 409 (duplicate username/email)

### GET /api/auth/me
Get the current authenticated user profile.

Response: `UserResponse`

### POST /api/auth/change-password
Change the current user's password.

Request: `{ "current_password": string, "new_password": string }`
Errors: 401 (wrong current password)

### POST /api/auth/generate-reset-token
Generate a one-time password reset token (60-minute expiry). Requires `auth.generate_reset` permission.

Request: `{ "user_id": string }`
Response: `{ "token": string, "expires_at": string }`

### POST /api/auth/use-reset-token
Use a reset token to set a new password.

Request: `{ "token": string, "new_password": string }`
Errors: 404 (not found), 410 (used or expired)

### POST /api/auth/request-deletion
Request soft-deletion of the current user's account (30-day hold).

### POST /api/auth/cancel-deletion
Cancel a pending account deletion.

### GET /api/auth/export-my-data
Export all of the current user's data as a downloadable JSON archive (profile, addresses, submissions, orders, reviews, cases).

### POST /api/auth/logout
Invalidate all active sessions for the current user.

---

## Users

### GET /api/users
List all users. Requires privileged access (staff/admin).

### GET /api/users/<id>
Get user profile. Users can access their own; privileged users can access any.

### PUT /api/users/profile
Update the current user's profile fields (first_name, last_name, username, contact_info, invoice_title).

### PUT /api/users/notification-prefs
Update the current user's notification preferences.

### PUT /api/users/<id>/role
Change a user's role. Requires `users.role_change` permission. Logged to audit trail.

### DELETE /api/users/<id>
Deactivate a user account. Requires administrator role and `users.manage` permission.

### GET /api/users/addresses
List shipping addresses for the current user.

### POST /api/users/addresses
Add a new shipping address.

### PUT /api/users/addresses/default
Set a default shipping address.

Request: `{ "address_id": string }`

### DELETE /api/users/addresses/<addr_id>
Remove an address.

### GET /api/users/notifications
Get notifications for the current user (most recent 50).

### PUT /api/users/notifications/<notif_id>/read
Mark a notification as read.

---

## Submissions

### GET /api/submissions/templates
Get the list of guided submission templates with type-specific required/optional fields.

### POST /api/submissions
Create a new submission draft. Requires `submissions.create` permission.

Request: `{ "title": string, "summary"?: string, "submission_type": string, "template_id"?: string, "deadline"?: string, "tags"?: string, "keywords"?: string }`

### GET /api/submissions
List submissions. Privileged users see all; others see own.

### GET /api/submissions/my
List the current user's submissions.

### GET /api/submissions/<id>
Get a submission with metadata.

### PUT /api/submissions/<id>
Update submission fields (title, summary, status, tags, keywords).

### POST /api/submissions/<id>/versions
Submit a new version (max 10 versions, before deadline).

Request: `{ "file_name": string, "file_data": string (base64), "form_data"?: string }`

### GET /api/submissions/<id>/versions
List all versions for a submission.

### GET /api/submissions/<id>/versions/<version>/download
Download a specific version. Watermark metadata included in response headers (`X-Watermark`, `X-Watermark-Hash`).

### POST /api/submissions/<id>/approve-blocked
Approve a submission blocked by sensitive word policy. Requires `submissions.approve_blocked` permission.

---

## Orders

### POST /api/orders
Create a new subscription order.

Request: `{ "subscription_period": "monthly|quarterly|annual", "shipping_address_id"?: string, "line_items": [{ "publication_title": string, "series_name"?: string, "quantity": int, "unit_price": float }] }`

### GET /api/orders
List orders. Privileged users see all; others see own.

### GET /api/orders/my
List the current user's orders.

### GET /api/orders/<id>
Get order details with line items.

### PUT /api/orders/<id>/status
Update order status. Requires privileged access.

Request: `{ "status": "pending|confirmed|processing|shipped|delivered|cancelled" }`

### POST /api/orders/split
Split an order by series into separate orders. Requires `orders.manage` permission.

Request: `{ "order_id": string }`

### POST /api/orders/merge
Merge multiple orders from the same user. Requires `orders.manage` permission.

Request: `{ "order_ids": [string] }`

### POST /api/orders/fulfillment
Log a fulfillment event. Requires `orders.fulfillment` permission. Automatically generates/updates reconciliation records.

Request: `{ "order_id": string, "line_item_id"?: string, "event_type": "missing_issue|reshipment|delay|discontinuation|edition_change|delivered", "issue_identifier"?: string, "reason": string, "expected_date"?: string, "actual_date"?: string }`

### GET /api/orders/<id>/fulfillment
List fulfillment events for an order.

### GET /api/orders/<id>/reconciliation
Get reconciliation records for an order (expected vs. received quantities).

### PUT /api/orders/reconciliation/<record_id>
Update a reconciliation record. Requires privileged access.

Request: `{ "received_qty": int, "notes"?: string }`

### POST /api/orders/clear-flag
Clear an abnormal order flag. Requires `orders.manage` permission.

Request: `{ "order_id": string }`

### GET /api/orders/flagged
List flagged orders. Requires `orders.manage` permission.

---

## Reviews

### POST /api/reviews
Post an initial review. Requires `reviews.create` permission.

Request: `{ "order_id": string, "line_item_id"?: string, "rating": int (1-5), "title": string (max 120 chars), "body": string }`

### POST /api/reviews/<id>/followup
Post a follow-up review (within 14 days, one per review).

### POST /api/reviews/<id>/images
Upload an image for a review (max 6 images, 5 MB each).

### GET /api/reviews
List reviews. Privileged users see all; others see own.

### GET /api/reviews/my
List the current user's reviews.

### GET /api/reviews/<id>
Get review details with images.

---

## After-Sales Cases

### POST /api/cases
Open a new after-sales case. Requires `cases.create` permission.

Request: `{ "order_id": string, "case_type": "return|refund|exchange", "subject": string, "description": string, "priority"?: "low|medium|high|urgent" }`

### GET /api/cases
List cases. Privileged users see all; others see own.

### GET /api/cases/my
List the current user's cases.

### GET /api/cases/<id>
Get case details with SLA timers (first_response_overdue, resolution_overdue, hours remaining).

### PUT /api/cases/<id>/status
Update case status. Requires `cases.manage` permission. Enforces valid status transitions.

Request: `{ "status": "in_review|awaiting_evidence|arbitrated|approved|denied|closed" }`

### POST /api/cases/<id>/assign
Assign a case to a staff member. Requires `cases.manage` permission.

Request: `{ "assigned_to": string }`

### POST /api/cases/<id>/comments
Add a comment to a case.

Request: `{ "content": string }`

### GET /api/cases/<id>/comments
Get comments for a case.

---

## Payments

### POST /api/payments
Record a payment transaction. Requires `payments.manage` permission. Idempotent via `idempotency_key`.

Request: `{ "order_id": string, "idempotency_key": string, "payment_method": "cash|check|on_account", "amount": float, "transaction_type": "charge|hold|release|refund", "reference_payment_id"?: string, "check_number"?: string, "notes"?: string }`

### POST /api/payments/refund
Issue a refund against a prior transaction. Requires `payments.manage` permission. Idempotent.

Request: `{ "original_payment_id": string, "idempotency_key": string, "amount": float, "reason"?: string }`

### GET /api/payments/order/<order_id>
List payment transactions for an order. Users can see their own orders; privileged users see all.

### GET /api/payments/reconciliation-report
Get the payment reconciliation report (charges, holds, refunds, balances). Requires `admin.dashboard` permission.

### GET /api/payments/abnormal-flags
List abnormal order flags. Requires `payments.manage` permission.

### POST /api/payments/abnormal-flags/<flag_id>/clear
Clear an abnormal flag. Requires `payments.manage` permission.

---

## Admin

### GET /api/admin/dashboard
Get dashboard statistics. Requires `admin.dashboard` permission.

### GET /api/admin/audit-log
Get audit log entries. Requires `admin.audit` permission.

### GET /api/admin/audit-logs
Alias for audit-log endpoint.

### GET /api/admin/settings
Get system settings. Requires `admin.settings` permission.

### POST /api/admin/cleanup-soft-deleted
Permanently delete users past the 30-day soft-delete hold. Requires `admin.settings` permission.

---

## Content Governance

### GET /api/content/sensitive-words
List sensitive words in the dictionary. Requires `content.manage` permission.

### POST /api/content/sensitive-words
Add a sensitive word with policy (replace or block). Requires `content.manage` permission.

Request: `{ "word": string, "action": "replace|block", "replacement"?: string }`

### DELETE /api/content/sensitive-words/<id>
Remove a sensitive word. Requires `content.manage` permission.

### POST /api/content/check
Check text against the sensitive word dictionary.

Request: `{ "text": string }`
Response: `{ "is_blocked": bool, "blocked_words": [string], "processed_text": string, "replacements_made": [string] }`

### POST /api/content/items/<id>/submit
Submit a content item for review.

### POST /api/content/items/<id>/approve
Approve a content item.

### POST /api/content/items/<id>/reject
Reject a content item.

### POST /api/content/items/<id>/request-revision
Request revision on a content item.

### POST /api/content/items/<id>/publish
Publish an approved content item.

### DELETE /api/content/items/<id>/rollback/<version>
Roll back a content item to a previous version.
"}

All 9 issues have been fixed across 15 files. Here's a summary of all changes:

### High Severity Fixes

1. **Frontend sign-out now invalidates server-side session** - `auth::logout()` is now async, calls `POST /api/auth/logout` before clearing localStorage. NavBar updated to use async logout with redirect.

2. **Reconciliation workflow fully implemented** - Added `generate_reconciliation_record()` that creates/updates records when fulfillment events are logged. Added `generate_initial_reconciliation()` that creates pending records at order creation. Both auto-generate records with correct expected/received quantities and status.

3. **RBAC: user deactivation restricted to administrators** - `deactivate_user` route now checks `auth.role == "administrator"` in addition to `users.manage` permission, preventing academic staff from deactivating users.

4. **API docs rewritten to match actual routes** - Completely rewrote `docs/api-spec.md` to accurately reflect all implemented endpoints including correct paths, methods, permissions, and request/response formats.

### Medium Severity Fixes

5. **Login/provision fail on session insert error** - Changed `let _ =` to `.map_err(|_| Status::InternalServerError)?` for session INSERT in both `login()` and `register()` handlers.

6. **Guided submission templates implemented** - Added `SubmissionTemplate` model with 4 built-in templates (journal article, conference paper, thesis, book chapter) with type-specific required/optional fields. Added `GET /api/submissions/templates` endpoint. Frontend form now has template selector that shows field guidance.

7. **Client-side route guards added** - Added `require_auth()` and `require_admin()` guard functions. All protected routes check auth before rendering; admin routes additionally check for administrator role.

8. **Reconciliation scheduler runs at midnight** - Replaced fixed 24h sleep with dynamic calculation of time until next midnight using `chrono::Local`.

### Low Severity Fix

9. **Session timeout defaults aligned** - Changed `docker-compose.yml` default from 60 to 30 minutes, matching `.env.example` and the runtime constant `SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES = 30`.

## Summary of Meridian Academy Project Exploration

I have thoroughly explored the Meridian Academy project and read all the requested files. Here's what I found:

### 1. **Frontend API Service** (`/repo/frontend/src/services/api.rs`)
- 130 lines total
- Implements HTTP client wrapper with functions: `get`, `post`, `post_empty`, `put`, `put_empty`, `post_no_body`, `delete`
- Handles authorization with Bearer tokens from localStorage
- Backend URL: `http://localhost:8000`
- All authenticated requests attach JWT tokens from `auth_token`

### 2. **API Specifications** (Two versions found)
- **`docs/api-spec.md`** - Root level specification (347 lines) - Comprehensive REST API spec with endpoints for auth, users, submissions, orders, reviews, cases, payments, content governance, and admin
- **`repo/docs/api-spec.md`** - Repository level specification (duplicate, slightly different formatting) - Same endpoints with slightly different presentation

Key endpoint categories:
- Authentication (login, provision, password reset)
- User management and addresses
- Submissions with versioning and watermarking
- Orders with fulfillment and reconciliation
- Reviews with follow-up capabilities
- After-sales cases with SLA timers
- Payment processing with idempotency
- Content governance with sensitive word filtering
- Admin dashboard and audit logs

### 3. **Unit Tests** (`/repo/unit_tests/src/lib.rs`)
- 435 lines of comprehensive unit tests
- Tests for auth (JWT, password hashing, session timeouts)
- Validation tests (title/summary/tag limits)
- SEO generation tests
- File validation tests (PDF, DOCX, PNG, JPG magic bytes)
- Case state transition validation
- Sensitive word filtering (replace/block actions)
- Business day SLA calculations
- IDOR logic tests
- Watermark integrity tests
- Payment idempotency tests
- All constants verified: SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=30, PASSWORD_RESET_EXPIRY_MINUTES=60, SOFT_DELETE_HOLD_DAYS=30, etc.

### 4. **API Integration Tests** (`/repo/API_tests/src/lib.rs`)
- 1172 lines of comprehensive integration tests
- Tests health check, auth, RBAC, validation, IDOR protection
- Submission lifecycle tests
- Order creation, splitting, merging, fulfillment
- Case management and comment authorization
- Review follow-up rules (only 1 per original, within 14 days)
- Review image upload validation (max 6 images, 5MB each, PNG/JPG only)
- Payment idempotency and refund limits
- **Critical: Download contract tests** - Verifies native file types returned (NOT ZIP), watermark headers present, file integrity

### 5. **Reviews Route** (`/repo/backend/src/routes/reviews.rs`)
- 236 lines covering complete review lifecycle
- **Line 63**: `create_followup` function - validates follow-up rules:
  - Follow-up must be within 14 days of original review creation
  - Only 1 follow-up allowed per original review (checks `parent_review_id`)
  - Cannot create follow-up on a follow-up
- IDOR enforcement: review author, order owner, or staff/admin can access
- Review image upload with file type validation
- Image limits: max 6 images, 5MB each, SHA-256 integrity checking

### 6. **Content Governance Route** (`/repo/backend/src/routes/content.rs`)
- 214 lines for content governance
- **Line 172**: `rollback_version` function - allows rolling back submissions to previous versions
- Sensitive word management (list, add, remove)
- Content item lifecycle: submit → review → approve/reject → publish
- Status transitions with proper authorization checks
- Audit logging for all actions

### 7. **Submissions Route** (`/repo/backend/src/routes/submissions.rs`)
- 736 lines with comprehensive submission management
- **Line 720**: `approve_blocked` function - staff can approve submissions blocked by sensitive word policy
- Full versioning support (max 10 versions per spec)
- Watermarking system for downloads:
  - PDF: appends watermark page with text
  - DOCX: injects visible red italic watermark paragraph
  - PNG: decodes, renders text banner, re-encodes
  - JPG: decodes, renders text banner at 90% quality
- File validation: PDF, DOCX, PNG, JPG with magic byte checking
- SHA-256 file hashing
- Watermark includes: requester name, user ID, timestamp (MM/DD/YYYY 12-hour format)
- IDOR protection on downloads and version history
- Content sensitivity checking blocks submissions with flagged words

### 8. **Project README** (`repo/README.md`)
- Setup instructions for local run and Docker
- Test running with `run_tests.sh`
- Default credentials: admin/admin123
- Services: MySQL 8, Rocket backend (8000), Dioxus frontend (8080)

### 9. **Root README** (`README.md`)
- High-level project overview
- Role-based access: Student, Instructor, Academic Staff, Administrator
- Full-stack application for academic content submissions and fulfillment
- Fully offline-capable with no external services
- Tech stack: Dioxus (WASM frontend), Rocket (REST API), MySQL 8

### 10. **Docker Compose** (`repo/docker-compose.yml`)
- **Frontend environment section (lines 52-65)**:
  - Build context: Dockerfile at `frontend/Dockerfile`
  - Container name: `meridian_frontend`
  - Port: 8080:8080
  - Environment: `BACKEND_URL: http://backend:8000`
  - Network: `meridian_net`
  - Depends on: backend service (healthy condition)

### Key Technical Highlights:

1. **Security Features**:
   - IDOR protection throughout (submission access, order access, case comments)
   - RBAC with permission-based access control
   - 30-minute idle session timeout
   - Salted bcrypt password hashing
   - JWT with configurable expiry

2. **File Handling**:
   - Magic byte validation for all uploads
   - Watermarking embedded directly in file bytes (not ZIP bundles)
   - SHA-256 integrity checking
   - Base64 encoding/decoding for API transfers

3. **Business Logic**:
   - Review follow-ups limited to 1 per review, within 14 days
   - Case SLA tracking (2 business days first response, 7 days resolution)
   - Order reconciliation and fulfillment events
   - Submission version control with rollback capability
   - Sensitive content blocking with optional approval override

1	# Meridian Academy API Specification
2	
3	Base URL: `http://localhost:8000`
4	
5	## Authentication
6	
7	All endpoints except `POST /api/auth/login`, `POST /api/auth/use-reset-token`, and `GET /health` require a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header.
8	
9	Sessions expire after 30 minutes of idle time. The idle timer is refreshed on each authenticated request.
10	
11	### Endpoints
12	
13	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
14	|--------|------|------|-------------|
15	| GET | `/health` | No | Health check |
16	| POST | `/api/auth/login` | No | Login with username/password |
17	| POST | `/api/auth/provision` | Admin | Provision a new user account |
18	| GET | `/api/auth/me` | Yes | Get current user profile |
19	| POST | `/api/auth/change-password` | Yes | Change own password |
20	| POST | `/api/auth/generate-reset-token` | Admin | Generate one-time password reset token (60 min expiry) |
21	| POST | `/api/auth/use-reset-token` | No | Use reset token to set new password |
22	| POST | `/api/auth/request-deletion` | Yes | Request 30-day soft-delete hold |
23	| POST | `/api/auth/cancel-deletion` | Yes | Cancel pending deletion |
24	| GET | `/api/auth/export-my-data` | Yes | Download own data as JSON archive |
25	| POST | `/api/auth/logout` | Yes | Invalidate current session |
26	
27	### POST /api/auth/login
28	```json
29	{ "username": "string", "password": "string" }
30	```
31	Response: `{ "token": "jwt", "user": { ... } }`
32	
33	### POST /api/auth/provision
34	```json
35	{ "username": "string", "email": "string", "password": "string", "first_name": "string", "last_name": "string", "role": "student|instructor|academic_staff|administrator" }
36	```
37	
38	### POST /api/auth/use-reset-token
39	```json
40	{ "token": "string", "new_password": "string" }
41	```
42	
43	## Users
44	
45	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
46	|--------|------|------|-------------|
47	| GET | `/api/users` | Admin/Staff | List all users |
48	| GET | `/api/users/<id>` | Owner/Admin/Staff | Get user by ID |
49	| PUT | `/api/users/profile` | Yes | Update own profile |
50	| PUT | `/api/users/notification-prefs` | Yes | Update notification preferences |
51	| PUT | `/api/users/<id>/role` | Admin | Change user role |
52	| DELETE | `/api/users/<id>` | Admin | Deactivate user |
53	| GET | `/api/users/addresses` | Yes | List own addresses |
54	| POST | `/api/users/addresses` | Yes | Add shipping address |
55	| PUT | `/api/users/addresses/default` | Yes | Set default address |
56	| DELETE | `/api/users/addresses/<id>` | Yes | Delete address |
57	| GET | `/api/users/notifications` | Yes | Get notification inbox |
58	| PUT | `/api/users/notifications/<id>/read` | Yes | Mark notification read |
59	
60	## Submissions
61	
62	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
63	|--------|------|------|-------------|
64	| POST | `/api/submissions` | Student/Instructor | Create submission (title <= 120 chars, summary <= 500 chars) |
65	| GET | `/api/submissions` | Yes | List submissions (scoped by role) |
66	| GET | `/api/submissions/<id>` | Owner/Admin/Staff | Get submission (IDOR enforced) |
67	| PUT | `/api/submissions/<id>` | Owner/Admin/Staff | Update submission |
68	| POST | `/api/submissions/<id>/versions` | Owner | Submit file version (max 10, PDF/DOCX/PNG/JPG, 25MB, magic-byte verified) |
69	| GET | `/api/submissions/<id>/versions` | Owner/Admin/Staff | List version history (timestamps in MM/DD/YYYY 12h format) |
70	| GET | `/api/submissions/<id>/versions/<n>/download` | Owner/Admin/Staff | Download with watermark |
71	| GET | `/api/submissions/my` | Yes | List own submissions |
72	| POST | `/api/submissions/<id>/approve` | Staff/Admin | Approve blocked submission |
73	
74	### POST /api/submissions/<id>/versions
75	```json
76	{ "file_name": "paper.pdf", "file_data": "base64...", "form_data": "optional JSON" }
77	```
78	
79	## Orders
80	
81	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
82	|--------|------|------|-------------|
83	| POST | `/api/orders` | Yes | Create order (monthly/quarterly/annual, multiple line items) |
84	| GET | `/api/orders` | Yes | List orders (scoped by role) |
85	| GET | `/api/orders/<id>` | Owner/Admin/Staff | Get order with line items |
86	| PUT | `/api/orders/<id>/status` | Admin/Staff | Update order status |
87	| GET | `/api/orders/my` | Yes | List own orders |
88	| POST | `/api/orders/split` | Admin/Staff | Split order by series |
89	| POST | `/api/orders/merge` | Admin/Staff | Merge orders from same user |
90	| POST | `/api/orders/fulfillment` | Admin/Staff | Log fulfillment event (reason required) |
91	| GET | `/api/orders/<id>/fulfillment` | Owner/Admin/Staff | List fulfillment events |
92	| GET | `/api/orders/<id>/reconciliation` | Owner/Admin/Staff | Get reconciliation records |
93	| PUT | `/api/orders/reconciliation/<id>` | Admin/Staff | Update reconciliation record |
94	| POST | `/api/orders/clear-flag` | Admin/Staff | Clear abnormal order flag |
95	| GET | `/api/orders/flagged` | Admin/Staff | List flagged orders |
96	
97	### POST /api/orders
98	```json
99	{ "subscription_period": "monthly|quarterly|annual", "shipping_address_id": "optional", "line_items": [{ "publication_title": "string", "series_name": "optional", "quantity": 1, "unit_price": 29.99 }] }
100	```
101	
102	## Reviews
103	
104	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
105	|--------|------|------|-------------|
106	| POST | `/api/reviews` | Yes | Create review (rating 1-5, title <= 120, order must be delivered) |
107	| POST | `/api/reviews/followup` | Yes | Follow-up review (1 per original, within 14 days) |
108	| GET | `/api/reviews` | Yes | List reviews (own for users, all for staff) |
109	| GET | `/api/reviews/<id>` | Owner/OrderOwner/Staff | Get review |
110	| POST | `/api/reviews/<id>/images` | Owner | Upload image (max 6, 5MB each) |
111	| GET | `/api/reviews/my` | Yes | List own reviews |
112	
113	## After-Sales Cases
114	
115	Status workflow: `submitted -> in_review -> awaiting_evidence -> arbitrated -> approved/denied -> closed`
116	
117	SLA: First response within 2 business days (skips weekends), resolution target 7 business days.
118	
119	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
120	|--------|------|------|-------------|
121	| POST | `/api/cases` | Yes | Create case (return/refund/exchange) |
122	| GET | `/api/cases` | Yes | List cases (scoped by role) |
123	| GET | `/api/cases/<id>` | Reporter/Staff | Get case with SLA timers |
124	| PUT | `/api/cases/<id>/status` | Staff/Admin | Transition case status |
125	| PUT | `/api/cases/<id>/assign` | Staff/Admin | Assign case to staff |
126	| POST | `/api/cases/<id>/comments` | Reporter/Assigned/Staff | Add comment (IDOR enforced) |
127	| GET | `/api/cases/<id>/comments` | Reporter/Staff | List comments |
128	| GET | `/api/cases/my` | Yes | List own cases |
129	
130	## Payments
131	
132	Methods: cash, check, on_account. Idempotent by `idempotency_key`. Third-party gateways disabled by default.
133	
134	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
135	|--------|------|------|-------------|
136	| POST | `/api/payments` | Staff/Admin | Create payment (charge/hold/release/refund) |
137	| POST | `/api/payments/refund` | Staff/Admin | Refund against prior payment (idempotent) |
138	| GET | `/api/payments/order/<id>` | Owner/Staff | List payments for order |
139	| GET | `/api/payments/reconciliation-report` | Admin | On-demand reconciliation report (also auto-generated nightly by background scheduler) |
140	| GET | `/api/payments/abnormal-flags` | Staff/Admin | List abnormal order flags |
141	| POST | `/api/payments/abnormal-flags/<id>/clear` | Staff/Admin | Clear flag |
142	
143	## Content Governance
144	
145	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
146	|--------|------|------|-------------|
147	| GET | `/api/content/sensitive-words` | Admin | List sensitive word dictionary |
148	| POST | `/api/content/sensitive-words` | Admin | Add word (replace/block) |
149	| DELETE | `/api/content/sensitive-words/<id>` | Admin | Remove word |
150	| POST | `/api/content/check` | Staff/Admin | Check text against dictionary |
151	| POST | `/api/content/items/<id>/submit` | Owner | Submit draft for review |
152	| POST | `/api/content/items/<id>/approve` | Staff/Admin | Approve content |
153	| POST | `/api/content/items/<id>/reject` | Staff/Admin | Reject content |
154	| POST | `/api/content/items/<id>/request-revision` | Staff/Admin | Request revision |
155	| POST | `/api/content/items/<id>/publish` | Staff/Admin | Publish accepted content |
156	| POST | `/api/content/items/<id>/rollback/<version>` | Owner/Staff/Admin | Rollback to previous version |
157	
158	## Admin

7	All endpoints except `POST /api/auth/login`, `POST /api/auth/use-reset-token`, and `GET /health` require a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header.

1	# Meridian Academy API Specification
2	
3	Base URL: `http://localhost:8000`
4	
5	## Authentication
6	
7	All endpoints except `POST /api/auth/login`, `POST /api/auth/use-reset-token`, and `GET /health` require a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header.
8	
9	Sessions expire after 30 minutes of idle time. The idle timer is refreshed on each authenticated request.
10	
11	### Endpoints
12	
13	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
14	|--------|------|------|-------------|
15	| GET | `/health` | No | Health check |
16	| POST | `/api/auth/login` | No | Login with username/password |
17	| POST | `/api/auth/provision` | Admin | Provision a new user account |
18	| GET | `/api/auth/me` | Yes | Get current user profile |
19	| POST | `/api/auth/change-password` | Yes | Change own password |
20	| POST | `/api/auth/generate-reset-token` | Admin | Generate one-time password reset token (60 min expiry) |
21	| POST | `/api/auth/use-reset-token` | No | Use reset token to set new password |
22	| POST | `/api/auth/request-deletion` | Yes | Request 30-day soft-delete hold |
23	| POST | `/api/auth/cancel-deletion` | Yes | Cancel pending deletion |
24	| GET | `/api/auth/export-my-data` | Yes | Download own data as JSON archive |
25	| POST | `/api/auth/logout` | Yes | Invalidate current session |
26	
27	### POST /api/auth/login
28	```json
29	{ "username": "string", "password": "string" }
30	```
31	Response: `{ "token": "jwt", "user": { ... } }`
32	
33	### POST /api/auth/provision
34	```json
35	{ "username": "string", "email": "string", "password": "string", "first_name": "string", "last_name": "string", "role": "student|instructor|academic_staff|administrator" }
36	```
37	
38	### POST /api/auth/use-reset-token
39	```json
40	{ "token": "string", "new_password": "string" }
41	```
42	
43	## Users
44	
45	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
46	|--------|------|------|-------------|
47	| GET | `/api/users` | Admin/Staff | List all users |
48	| GET | `/api/users/<id>` | Owner/Admin/Staff | Get user by ID |
49	| PUT | `/api/users/profile` | Yes | Update own profile |
50	| PUT | `/api/users/notification-prefs` | Yes | Update notification preferences |
51	| PUT | `/api/users/<id>/role` | Admin | Change user role |
52	| DELETE | `/api/users/<id>` | Admin | Deactivate user |
53	| GET | `/api/users/addresses` | Yes | List own addresses |
54	| POST | `/api/users/addresses` | Yes | Add shipping address |
55	| PUT | `/api/users/addresses/default` | Yes | Set default address |
56	| DELETE | `/api/users/addresses/<id>` | Yes | Delete address |
57	| GET | `/api/users/notifications` | Yes | Get notification inbox |
58	| PUT | `/api/users/notifications/<id>/read` | Yes | Mark notification read |
59	
60	## Submissions
61	
62	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
63	|--------|------|------|-------------|
64	| GET | `/api/submissions/templates` | No | List guided submission templates with type-specific fields |
65	| POST | `/api/submissions` | Student/Instructor | Create submission (title <= 120 chars, summary <= 500 chars) |
66	| GET | `/api/submissions` | Yes | List submissions (scoped by role) |
67	| GET | `/api/submissions/<id>` | Owner/Admin/Staff | Get submission (IDOR enforced) |
68	| PUT | `/api/submissions/<id>` | Owner/Admin/Staff | Update submission |
69	| POST | `/api/submissions/<id>/versions` | Owner | Submit file version (max 10, PDF/DOCX/PNG/JPG, 25MB, magic-byte verified) |
70	| GET | `/api/submissions/<id>/versions` | Owner/Admin/Staff | List version history (timestamps in MM/DD/YYYY 12h format) |
71	| GET | `/api/submissions/<id>/versions/<n>/download` | Owner/Admin/Staff | Download with watermark |
72	| GET | `/api/submissions/my` | Yes | List own submissions |
73	| POST | `/api/submissions/<id>/approve` | Staff/Admin | Approve blocked submission |
74	
75	### POST /api/submissions/<id>/versions
76	```json
77	{ "file_name": "paper.pdf", "file_data": "base64...", "form_data": "optional JSON" }
78	```
79	
80	## Orders
81	
82	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
83	|--------|------|------|-------------|
84	| POST | `/api/orders` | Yes | Create order (monthly/quarterly/annual, multiple line items) |
85	| GET | `/api/orders` | Yes | List orders (scoped by role) |
86	| GET | `/api/orders/<id>` | Owner/Admin/Staff | Get order with line items |
87	| PUT | `/api/orders/<id>/status` | Admin/Staff | Update order status |
88	| GET | `/api/orders/my` | Yes | List own orders |
89	| POST | `/api/orders/split` | Admin/Staff | Split order by series |
90	| POST | `/api/orders/merge` | Admin/Staff | Merge orders from same user |
91	| POST | `/api/orders/fulfillment` | Admin/Staff | Log fulfillment event (reason required) |
92	| GET | `/api/orders/<id>/fulfillment` | Owner/Admin/Staff | List fulfillment events |
93	| GET | `/api/orders/<id>/reconciliation` | Owner/Admin/Staff | Get reconciliation records |
94	| PUT | `/api/orders/reconciliation/<id>` | Admin/Staff | Update reconciliation record |
95	| POST | `/api/orders/clear-flag` | Admin/Staff | Clear abnormal order flag |
96	| GET | `/api/orders/flagged` | Admin/Staff | List flagged orders |
97	
98	### POST /api/orders
99	```json
100	{ "subscription_period": "monthly|quarterly|annual", "shipping_address_id": "optional", "line_items": [{ "publication_title": "string", "series_name": "optional", "quantity": 1, "unit_price": 29.99 }] }
101	```
102	
103	## Reviews
104	
105	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
106	|--------|------|------|-------------|
107	| POST | `/api/reviews` | Yes | Create review (rating 1-5, title <= 120, order must be delivered) |
108	| POST | `/api/reviews/followup` | Yes | Follow-up review (1 per original, within 14 days) |
109	| GET | `/api/reviews` | Yes | List reviews (own for users, all for staff) |
110	| GET | `/api/reviews/<id>` | Owner/OrderOwner/Staff | Get review |
111	| POST | `/api/reviews/<id>/images` | Owner | Upload image (max 6, 5MB each) |
112	| GET | `/api/reviews/my` | Yes | List own reviews |
113	
114	## After-Sales Cases
115	
116	Status workflow: `submitted -> in_review -> awaiting_evidence -> arbitrated -> approved/denied -> closed`
117	
118	SLA: First response within 2 business days (skips weekends), resolution target 7 business days.
119	
120	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
121	|--------|------|------|-------------|
122	| POST | `/api/cases` | Yes | Create case (return/refund/exchange) |
123	| GET | `/api/cases` | Yes | List cases (scoped by role) |
124	| GET | `/api/cases/<id>` | Reporter/Staff | Get case with SLA timers |
125	| PUT | `/api/cases/<id>/status` | Staff/Admin | Transition case status |
126	| PUT | `/api/cases/<id>/assign` | Staff/Admin | Assign case to staff |
127	| POST | `/api/cases/<id>/comments` | Reporter/Assigned/Staff | Add comment (IDOR enforced) |
128	| GET | `/api/cases/<id>/comments` | Reporter/Staff | List comments |
129	| GET | `/api/cases/my` | Yes | List own cases |
130	
131	## Payments
132	
133	Methods: cash, check, on_account. Idempotent by `idempotency_key`. Third-party gateways disabled by default.
134	
135	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
136	|--------|------|------|-------------|
137	| POST | `/api/payments` | Staff/Admin | Create payment (charge/hold/release/refund) |
138	| POST | `/api/payments/refund` | Staff/Admin | Refund against prior payment (idempotent) |
139	| GET | `/api/payments/order/<id>` | Owner/Staff | List payments for order |
140	| GET | `/api/payments/reconciliation-report` | Admin | On-demand reconciliation report (also auto-generated nightly by background scheduler) |
141	| GET | `/api/payments/abnormal-flags` | Staff/Admin | List abnormal order flags |
142	| POST | `/api/payments/abnormal-flags/<id>/clear` | Staff/Admin | Clear flag |
143	
144	## Content Governance
145	
146	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
147	|--------|------|------|-------------|
148	| GET | `/api/content/sensitive-words` | Admin | List sensitive word dictionary |
149	| POST | `/api/content/sensitive-words` | Admin | Add word (replace/block) |
150	| DELETE | `/api/content/sensitive-words/<id>` | Admin | Remove word |
151	| POST | `/api/content/check` | Staff/Admin | Check text against dictionary |
152	| POST | `/api/content/items/<id>/submit` | Owner | Submit draft for review |
153	| POST | `/api/content/items/<id>/approve` | Staff/Admin | Approve content |
154	| POST | `/api/content/items/<id>/reject` | Staff/Admin | Reject content |
155	| POST | `/api/content/items/<id>/request-revision` | Staff/Admin | Request revision |
156	| POST | `/api/content/items/<id>/publish` | Staff/Admin | Publish accepted content |
157	| POST | `/api/content/items/<id>/rollback/<version>` | Owner/Staff/Admin | Rollback to previous version |
158	
159	## Admin
160	
161	| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
162	|--------|------|------|-------------|
163	| GET | `/api/admin/dashboard` | Admin | Dashboard statistics |
164	| GET | `/api/admin/audit-log` | Admin | Immutable audit log (last 200 entries, returns `{ "logs": [...] }`) |
165	| GET | `/api/admin/audit-logs` | Admin | Same as above, returns flat array (used by frontend) |
166	| GET | `/api/admin/settings` | Admin | System settings and constants |
167	| POST | `/api/admin/cleanup-soft-deleted` | Admin | Permanently delete expired soft-deleted users |

1	# Meridian Academic Publishing & Fulfillment Portal
2	
3	A full-stack, offline-first web application for academic institutions managing content submissions, journal order fulfillment, peer reviews, and after-sales case management.
4	
5	
6	
7	
8	## Quick Start
9	
10	```bash
11	cd repo
12	docker compose up
13	# Backend: http://localhost:8000
14	# Frontend: http://localhost:8080
15	```
16	
17	> No configuration needed — all defaults are built in. Optionally copy `.env.example` to `.env` to customize credentials.
18	
19	## Running Tests
20	
21	```bash
22	cd repo
23	./run_tests.sh
24	```
25	
26	│
27	## Tech Stack
28	
29	- **Frontend**: Dioxus (Rust → WebAssembly via Trunk)
30	- **Backend**: Rocket (Rust REST API)
31	- **Database**: MySQL 8
32	- **Auth**: Local-only, salted password hashing, 30-min idle session timeout
33	- **Offline**: Fully runnable on local network, no external services required
